title: Boolean operations should avoid having numeric operands, and vice versa
description: There are several constructs in the language that work with boolean: If statements: if (b) ... Conditional operator: int i = b ? 0 : 42; Logical operators: (b1 || b2) && !b3 Those operations would also work with arithmetic or enum values operands, because there is a conversion from those types to bool. However, this conversion might not always be obvious, for instance, an integer return code might use the value 0 to indicate that everything worked as expected, but converted to boolean, this value would be false, which often denotes failure.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Avoid mixing boolean and numeric operands in operations.
